title: Monitor health assessment
description: What the supervisory monitor writes to runs/<run>/monitor/assessment.json each time it wakes. The monitor judges the HEALTH OF THE RUN (process), not the artifact. It writes only under monitor/; the orchestrator reads this + monitor/HALT at its checkpoints and is the sole writer of STATE.json. See DESIGN §10.1.

assessment.description: healthy = system working, continue. warning = something to surface but not halt. intervene = should be stopped (only acts in enforcing mode, above the confidence floor).

confidence.description: Confidence in the assessment. In enforcing mode, an 'intervene' only halts when confidence >= monitor.intervene_min_confidence.

recommended_action.description: What the monitor recommends. Only 'halt' (in enforcing mode, above the confidence floor) writes monitor/HALT.

halt_requested.description: Mirror of whether monitor/HALT was written this cycle. The orchestrator treats the presence of monitor/HALT as authoritative.

progressing.description: Is the run making genuine progress (blockers trending down / stage advancing) vs spinning?

observed.description: Snapshot the monitor read from the blackboard.

summary.description: One-paragraph health summary for the human report.

evidence.description: TRUSTED-telemetry support: STATE.json round/time/error counts, repeated content-keys, verdict JSON — NOT LOG.md or prose. A halt recommendation must cite this (DESIGN §10.1).

rationale.description: Why this assessment — especially required (non-empty) when recommending halt; must cite trusted telemetry.
